description: Tissue-specific targeting and delivery optimization for genetic medicines - ligands, formulations, and biodistribution.

facts.context: GalNAc (N-acetylgalactosamine) conjugation enables hepatocyte targeting by:
facts.truth: binding asialoglycoprotein receptor (ASGPR) highly expressed on hepatocytes

facts.context: Transferrin receptor targeting for brain delivery works because:
facts.truth: TfR is expressed on brain endothelium and mediates transcytosis across BBB
distractors: TfR is only in peripheral tissues

facts.context: Antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) achieve tumor selectivity through:
facts.truth: antibody binding to tumor antigen followed by internalization and payload release

facts.context: LNPs naturally accumulate in liver because:
facts.truth: they acquire ApoE in circulation which targets hepatocyte LDL receptors
distractors: liver has no selectivity for LNPs
LNPs are too large for other tissues

facts.context: LNP delivery to spleen and immune cells can be enhanced by:
facts.truth: incorporating anionic lipids or modifying PEG-lipid ratios

facts.context: AAV serotypes with muscle tropism (AAV8, AAV9) achieve this by:
facts.truth: binding specific cell surface receptors/co-receptors on muscle fibers

facts.context: Intrathecal delivery of gene therapies for CNS diseases:
facts.truth: bypasses blood-brain barrier, achieving CSF and spinal cord distribution
distractors: only treats brain, not spinal cord

facts.context: Subretinal injection for retinal gene therapy:
facts.truth: directly accesses RPE and photoreceptors with minimal systemic exposure
particles >100nm are cleared by liver/spleen; <10nm are renally cleared
distractors: size has no effect on distribution

facts.context: Endosomal escape is critical for nucleic acid delivery because:
facts.truth: nucleic acids must reach cytoplasm (mRNA) or nucleus (DNA) to function
distractors: endosomes are the site of action
